text: One of the reasons that you're not
getting as many leads as you want is
fundamentally because not enough people
know about your stuff. And it's very
easy for me to say this, but it's like
you have to really internalize how
unbelievably large the market is and how
small and and ephemeral our attempts at
being known are. And so I'll tell you
what happened at the last launch two
years ago. I had people still coming up
to me on the street and 19 out of 20
who'd come up to me, I'd say, "Hey, you
coming to the book launch?" And they'd
be like, "I didn't even know you wrote a
book." And I'd already had offers out at
that point and I was, you know,
advertising my face off for last launch
and no one knew. Now, this time I had a
slightly higher percentage, probably
like one of four had heard that I had a
book launch coming and three out of four
still didn't. And I say that having
spent like $4 or5 million. And these are
people who already know who I was. still
didn't even know that I had a book
launch coming. And again, I I bring this
up because so I I so many times I hear
the like, I think I've saturated my
market. I think I don't think there's
any more people who could find out. And
it's like, you're just so wrong. And I I
just I want to give as many of examples
to blow that blow that belief out of the
water as I possibly can. And so to to
further reinforce this, you guys may not
know this, but a month before the lead's
launch last year, we actually had a
leak. And so what happened was one of,
you know, one of the one of the people
on my team was kind of getting
everything ready on Amazon uh to launch
the book, obviously, you know, release
it. And the way Amazon books works, I
won't bore you with it, but you have to
basically upload the book ahead of time.
And they accidentally set it up as live.
And so they didn't realize that they had
done that. And so for 24 hours, my leads
book, which I had been keeping in secret
and I've been, you know, preparing all
this like whisper tea shot, all this
stuff. 72 people bought the book in like
60 minutes
and I was, you know, a month plus out
and I was like, the launch is ruined,
you know, like, oh my, like the book's
going to be out there, like the whole
thing, right? Here's the funniest part
of all of it. Nothing happened. And I'll
tell you, I'll tell you what what gave
me peace at night was I realized that
somebody would have to take the book and
then they would have to begin promoting
it. They would have to start advertising
it because I was out here shouting from
the rooftops that my book was coming
out. And it was this big event, this big
thing. And still 19 out of 20 people had
no idea who followed me, that I even had
a book. And so this these 72 random
strangers who who did actually buy the
book ahead of time, I saw one guy
commented on a LinkedIn post. He was
like, "How is this somehow already in my
Kindle?" And I was just like, "No one
comment on this guy's saying." And um
but as a result, it was this really it
was this very valuable lesson for me of
like
no one knows you exist. No one knows
your business exists. No one knows what
you're up to. Everyone's obsessed with
their own lives. And it's like we have
to put so much effort into becoming
known um that we we just typically
wildly under underestimate the effort.
That's all I can say is that like I knew
that it would be a lot of work to to
build the brand that we have now and it
still pales in comparison to how much
work it's actually been.
